title: Force FAL sprite-sheet generation from references
description: Sprite Builder sheet output currently sends the user's raw prompt to FAL and can return a single sprite instead of a grid. Sheet output must augment the hidden provider prompt with rows/cols, target list, transparent grid constraints, and reference-character preservation. Edit/image-to-image modes must use a compatible edit/image endpoint so uploaded references actually condition the result.
close_reason: Forced reference sprite-sheet requests onto edit endpoints with expanded sheet prompt and payload smoke coverage

title: Advertise endpoint capabilities from hub surfaces accurately
description:
Summary:
Make every hub endpoint advertise current capabilities accurately so PSFN can compute effective permissions from registry maximums and current device state.

Scope:
- Normalize capability profiles for Pi realtime client, Python ESPHome fallback, Voxta/avatar facade, Device Studio live simulated satellite, and thin shell.
- Include capability details for input/output/control/safety plus semantic domains: vision, avatar/display/expression/action, audio input/output, STT/TTS, telemetry, location/timezone, mobility/static state, and future robotics denied/absent state.
- Allow runtime degradation: if a camera, microphone, speaker, avatar action, or telemetry source is unavailable, advertise the reduced current capability set.
- Capture capability state in local artifacts/logs for debugging.

Acceptance:
- Each endpoint path has a test proving its advertised capabilities match what that endpoint can actually do.
- Capability changes do not require changing PSFN framework code; they are claim/profile data.
- Device Studio and Voxta do not advertise vision/action support unless the corresponding path is implemented or clearly marked as planned/denied.

title: Send registry-protocol satellite claims from TS and Python runtime adapters
description:
Summary:
Update hub PSFN request adapters to send the structured satellite claim envelope and current capability advertisement expected by the framework registry protocol.

Scope:
- Update TypeScript PsfnModelAdapter to include the claim envelope and capability metadata in the agreed header/body shape once the framework contract is finalized.
- Update Python PsfnStreamingProvider to use the same claim envelope and capability metadata for ESPHome/realtime fallback paths.
- Remove hardcoded authoritative claim types such as openhome or psfn-satellite-hub from request construction.
- Preserve stable session/thread IDs so PSFN can maintain L0 continuity per satellite session.
- Make error reporting clear when PSFN denies a claim or capability.

Acceptance:
- TS and Python tests assert the exact claim/capability fields sent to PSFN.
- Existing local hub websocket protocol keeps working while the PSFN-side claim envelope changes.
- Framework denial errors are surfaced in hub logs/artifacts without leaking secrets.
- Hub does not treat endpoint-advertised capabilities as granted permissions; it only reports them.
close_reason: Completed and validated: TypeScript PsfnModelAdapter and Python PsfnStreamingProvider now send framework registry-protocol scalar headers for claim type, satellite ID, endpoint ID, session/thread, canonical capabilities, telemetry scopes, and optional cert fingerprint while keeping the JSON envelope as diagnostic body metadata. Hub capability profiles now map to the framework vocabulary and mobile-location includes speech plus vision-capable defaults for the Android POC. Gates passed: npm run check:ts, npm run test:ts, PYTHONPATH=. uv run pytest, git diff --check.

title: Add mTLS or cert-bound PSFN connection support
description:
Summary:
Add hub support for secure endpoint identity when connecting to PSFN, targeting mTLS/client certificates or equivalent cert-bound authentication.

Scope:
- Add TypeScript PSFN runtime adapter support for client cert/key/CA configuration when calling PSFN over HTTPS.
- Add Python httpx client support for client cert/key/CA configuration for the ESPHome fallback path.
- Keep bearer API key support only as a bootstrap/compat path where explicitly configured; do not treat it as sufficient for high-trust registered satellite claims if framework policy requires cert identity.
- Validate cert config fail-closed: missing key/cert pairs, unreadable files, invalid CA path, and insecure combinations should error clearly.
- Ensure secret paths and key material are not logged.

Acceptance:
- TS and Python adapters can be configured to present client cert identity to PSFN.
- Tests cover cert option construction and failure modes without committing real secrets.
- Docs explain expected PSFN-side registry binding: cert identity maps to registered endpoint claims.

title: Define hub endpoint claim and capability configuration
description:
Summary:
Define hub-side configuration for satellite endpoint identity, claim envelopes, and advertised capabilities across TypeScript and Python paths.

Scope:
- Define config shape for endpoint classes: Pi realtime voice, Python ESPHome fallback, Voxta/avatar, Device Studio simulated satellite, thin shell/text-only, telemetry-only, and future mobile/robotics endpoints.
- Include claim namespace/type, satellite ID, endpoint ID, device class, display name, session/thread derivation, static/mobile location mode, and current capability profile.
- Define capability vocabulary aligned with the framework plan: text, microphone/audio input, STT, TTS/audio output, vision/camera/image upload, avatar/display/expression/action, future robotics, location/timezone, presence, health/battery, telemetry, and outbound delivery.
- Make default examples safe and explicit; avoid implying capabilities that the endpoint cannot actually support.

Acceptance:
- TypeScript config loader and Python runtime config can represent the endpoint claim/capability fields needed by the protocol.
- Existing .env.example and docs describe the intended config without hardcoding one universal channel type.
- Tests cover at least voice-only, Voxta/avatar, text-only, and telemetry/mobile-location config examples.

title: Implement secure satellite claim registry protocol in the hub
description:
Summary:
Update psfn-satellite-hub to speak the upcoming PSFN secure satellite claim registry protocol instead of chasing a fixed framework channel type such as psfn-amica.

Problem:
The previous bead framed the fix as renaming hub claims to match the current PSFN hardcoded external channel type. That is the wrong direction. Different satellite endpoint types need different claim namespaces and capabilities: Pi voice endpoints, Voxta/avatar endpoints, browser/mobile satellites, future robotics endpoints, telemetry-only sensors, and sidecars. The hub should present registered endpoint claims securely and advertise current capabilities, while PSFN validates those claims against its registry and computes effective permissions.

Target shape:
- Hub sends a structured satellite claim envelope: claim type/namespace, satellite ID, endpoint ID, session/thread ID, endpoint/device class, static/mobile status, current capabilities, and optional telemetry freshness.
- Hub connects to PSFN using secure endpoint identity, preferably mTLS/client cert validation or equivalent cert-bound auth material.
- Hub never assumes raw advertised capabilities grant permission. It advertises current endpoint capabilities and expects PSFN to intersect those with registry max capabilities.
- Hub supports capability profiles for voice-only, text-only, Voxta/avatar, vision-capable, telemetry-only, and mobile-location endpoints.
- Hub forwards telemetry such as location/timezone/presence/battery/health only when configured for that endpoint.
- TypeScript hub, Python ESPHome fallback, thin shell, Device Studio simulated satellite, and Voxta facade should converge on the same claim/capability vocabulary where practical.

Acceptance:
- Hub no longer hardcodes openhome or psfn-satellite-hub as authoritative PSFN external claim types.
- Hub configuration can describe endpoint claim identity and certificate/auth settings per endpoint class.
- Hub requests include a registry-protocol-compatible claim envelope and capability advertisement.
- Hub can use mTLS/client cert material or equivalent cert-bound auth when talking to PSFN.
- Telemetry and capabilities are explicit, configurable, and test-covered.
- Integration examples match the PSFN framework epic PSFN-mztb once that side lands.
- npm run test:ts and uv run pytest pass before closure.

Framework dependency:
This hub work depends on the PSFN framework accepting registry-backed satellite claims. Track framework side under PSFN-mztb and its children.

comments.author: axAilotl
comments.text: Implemented foundational satellite claim registry support in hub: TS/Python claim envelopes, configurable endpoint identity/capability profiles/telemetry advertisement, client cert config and TS/Python mTLS-capable request paths, updated defaults/docs from psfn-satellite-hub/openhome to satellite.endpoint, and tests for request payloads/config. Quality gates: npm run test:ts; uv run python -m pytest; PYTHONPATH-adjusted uv run pytest due user PATH shadowing.

title: Load Device Studio FAL key from .env
description: Device Studio server should load local .env so FAL_KEY provided for sprite generation is picked up when running the LAN server. Do not commit the secret; .env remains gitignored.
close_reason: Device Studio server imports dotenv/config and local .env now contains the FAL key outside git

title: Fix Sprite Builder overflow and reference-image upload
description: Fix the Device Studio Sprite Builder so form controls do not overflow beneath adjacent review/approved panels, and add real reference-image plus full sprite-sheet workflows for image generation. Reference uploads must be distinct from importing finished candidate sprites: uploaded reference art should preview in the controls, feed /api/sprites/generate as imageUrl/imageUrls for image-to-image/edit, and not be auto-assigned to an expression or viseme candidate. The builder must also support generating or importing an entire sprite sheet, selecting rows/columns and frame IDs, slicing the sheet into approved expression/viseme frames, then packing/exporting through the existing deterministic packer. Manual single-frame import should still allow explicit target assignment through the selected kind/target controls.
close_reason: Implemented reference uploads, sheet generation/import slicing, overflow fix, and browser smoke coverage

title: Implement Device Studio hub websocket client
description:
Implement the client-side adapter that lets Device Studio connect to the existing realtime hub websocket as a simulated satellite. It should send hello with device/profile capabilities, support user.text turns, interrupt, ping/pong, and consume hub message/audio/text lifecycle events.

It must support both live hub mode and mock mode. Mock mode should exercise the UI and behavior system without requiring Deepgram, ElevenLabs, PSFN, Hermes, or physical hardware. The adapter should preserve the existing hub protocol and only introduce new messages behind a documented extension if necessary.
acceptance_criteria: Device Studio can connect to a running hub or mock transport. It receives assistant/user message events, reflects connection state, sends typed turns and interrupts, logs all structured events, and has tests for handshake/message parsing behavior. Existing hub tests continue to pass.
close_reason: Completed Device Studio hub/mock websocket client on agents/device-studio-hubclient

title: Implement behavior library, import/export, and playback engine
description:
Build the behavior subsystem for loading behavior definitions, listing them by profile/channel/provenance, importing/exporting JSON, and playing frames through time. It should support movement + expression timelines, expression-only one-frame behaviors, source labels, generated-vs-official provenance, and hardware verification state.

The playback engine should emit normalized render state at a given timestamp so browser previews, tests, and future firmware exporters can share the same interpretation.
acceptance_criteria: Behavior fixtures load into a library, can be imported/exported as JSON, and can be played deterministically. Tests cover duration, frame ordering, sampling/interpolation policy, profile/channel filtering, and event emission for frame apply/playback start/stop.
close_reason: Completed Device Studio behavior library/playback engine on agents/device-studio-behavior

title: Add Device Studio app scaffold and npm scripts
description:
Add a repo-local Device Studio application scaffold suitable for browser-based simulation and authoring. It should be clearly separated from core hub code, with scripts to run and build it. Keep dependency choices conservative and compatible with the existing TypeScript/npm setup.

The first screen should be the actual studio surface, not a marketing page: preview area, profile selector, live/mock status, behavior/timeline area, and command/event log placeholders.
acceptance_criteria: A documented npm script launches Device Studio locally. The scaffold builds under the repo TypeScript/tooling conventions. The initial UI has stable regions for preview, controls, behavior list, and event log without changing production hub startup.
close_reason: Completed Device Studio app scaffold on agents/device-studio-app

title: Add Stack-chan and Waveshare ESP32 device profiles
description:
Add concrete profile data for the first two target device classes. Stack-chan should model screen, face, touch, speaker/mic, RGB LED, and two servo movement channels where supported by the reference hardware. The Waveshare ESP32-S3 1.85 inch round LCD board should model a 360x360 round screen, touch capability where present, and screen-centric output capabilities.

Profiles should include source/provenance notes and hardware verification status. Avoid pretending untested movement ranges are hardware-safe; mark them as unverified until measured on a real unit.
acceptance_criteria: Profile fixtures exist for Stack-chan and Waveshare round LCD. Tests or validation checks confirm profiles load, expose capabilities correctly, and degrade behavior channels appropriately. The data includes source/provenance and verification fields.
close_reason: Completed concrete Stack-chan and Waveshare profiles on agents/device-studio-profiles-clean

title: Device Studio architecture and protocol contract
description:
Write a self-contained architecture document for the ESP32 companion Device Studio. It should explain why this is a behavioral simulator rather than a CPU/peripheral emulator, what belongs in tooling versus core hub runtime, how live/mock modes work, and how the tool talks to the existing realtime websocket hub.

The contract must cover device profiles, behavior timelines, expression/viseme state, movement channels, structured command/event logging, provenance, and hardware verification states. It should explicitly call out compatibility with Stack-chan-style servo/screen devices and screen-only ESP32 devices like the Waveshare round board.
acceptance_criteria: A docs page exists and describes the tool boundaries, live/mock flows, data model concepts, websocket message usage, protocol extension candidates, testing strategy, and MVP/non-goals. It must be detailed enough that implementation workers do not need to consult the original chat screenshot or conversation.
close_reason: Completed Device Studio architecture/protocol contract on agents/device-studio-spec

title: Define device profile and behavior timeline model
description:
Create TypeScript types and validation helpers for Device Studio profiles and behavior timelines. The model should represent screen dimensions/shape, touch capability, input/output capabilities, movement joints, expression/viseme channels, LEDs/backlight, provenance, hardware verification status, and timeline frames.

The model must be device-neutral: the same semantic behavior should be renderable on Stack-chan with servo motion and on a smaller LCD-only ESP32 by ignoring unsupported channels without failing. Include fixtures for representative behavior data such as neutral, happy/laughing, angry, and a short dance/sing-along style timeline.
acceptance_criteria: TypeScript model code and focused tests exist. Tests cover required fields, degradation on unsupported channels, timeline ordering/duration calculation, provenance labels, and hardware verification state. The code is reusable by both CLI/headless tools and browser UI.
close_reason: Completed shared Device Studio profile/behavior model on agents/device-studio-model

title: Build ESP32 companion Device Studio and simulator tooling
description:
Build repo-local tooling for ESP32-class embodied companion devices. The goal is a Device Studio that can connect to the existing PSFN Satellite Hub as a simulated satellite, preview device-specific screens/motion, author behavior timelines, and export assets/behavior data for later firmware work. This is intentionally tooling, not core hub runtime: it should live outside the production transport path while reusing the existing realtime websocket protocol and satellite capability vocabulary.

Targets include Stack-chan-style devices with screen, face, servo movement, LEDs, and app-server-controlled behaviors, plus smaller/lower-power ESP32 screens such as the Waveshare ESP32-S3 1.85 inch round LCD board. The authoring model should let one semantic behavior degrade cleanly across devices: Stack-chan can use movement + expression + viseme; a round LCD may use only screen/expression/viseme/touch; larger clients can later map the same semantic state to Three.js.

Important product shape: mirror the Stack-chan simulator screenshot pattern: profile selector, live/mock backend mode, command/event log, behavior library, timeline/frame playback, provenance labels such as official/device-derived vs host-generated, and a hardware verification state so host-generated movement is not treated as proven until tested on a real unit.
acceptance_criteria: Epic is complete when Device Studio can be launched from this repo, connect to a local hub or mock backend, simulate at least Stack-chan and Waveshare round LCD profiles, play/import/export behavior timelines, render expression/viseme state, log structured commands/events, and document the hardware verification workflow. Production hub behavior must remain unchanged unless a deliberately documented protocol extension is added with tests.
notes:
Execution plan created 2026-05-11:

Layer 0 can run in parallel:
- psfn-bsy.1 architecture/protocol contract, assigned to worktree agents/device-studio-spec.
- psfn-bsy.2 shared profile/behavior model and tests, assigned to worktree agents/device-studio-model.
- psfn-bsy.3 browser Device Studio scaffold and npm scripts, assigned to worktree agents/device-studio-app.

Layer 1 unlocks after model/scaffold:
- psfn-bsy.4 concrete Stack-chan and Waveshare profiles depends on psfn-bsy.2.
- psfn-bsy.5 hub websocket client depends on psfn-bsy.2 and psfn-bsy.3.
- psfn-bsy.6 behavior library/playback/import-export depends on psfn-bsy.2 and psfn-bsy.3.

Layer 2+ then builds renderers, authoring, command log, protocol extension tests, exports, docs, and final QA. Keep generated/unverified behavior distinct from hardware-verified behavior throughout.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Validate Voxta facade against AcidBubbles plugin
description: Run the new SignalR-compatible facade against the actual VaM/AcidBubbles Voxta plugin, capture any protocol mismatches, and add compatibility fixtures for the observed framing.

title: Add Voxta-compatible satellite routes
description: Implement the first Voxta-compatible facade for VaM/AcidBubbles-style plugin integration: SignalR-like hub route, message framing, capability/auth handshake, and routing into the existing PSFN embodied session seam.
close_reason: Implemented initial Voxta-compatible SignalR facade and route wiring

title: Add Hermes API-server runtime adapter
description: Use the latest hermes-agent OpenAI-compatible API server as the hub-facing Hermes runtime path, preserving stable hub session ids without relying on private in-process Hermes adapter internals.
close_reason: Implemented Hermes API-server runtime adapter with runtime selection and tests

title: Add PSFN embodied channel seam
description: Introduce the initial hub channel boundary for PSFN integration so the thin shell/test surface can exercise PSFN-backed turns through a stable channel identity.
close_reason: Implemented initial PSFN embodied channel seam, typed thin-shell turn path, and tests

title: Write satellite build guide for hub integration
description: Add a guide document explaining how to build a satellite endpoint that interfaces with the PSFN Satellite Hub, including websocket handshake, capability profiles, claim identity config, telemetry, and testing.
close_reason: Added docs/building-satellites.md and linked it from README.

title: Add hub/framework satellite protocol integration tests
description:
Summary:
Add hub-side tests and smoke fixtures that prove the hub speaks the framework satellite registry protocol cleanly once PSFN framework support lands.

Scope:
- TS unit tests for claim envelope construction, mTLS config, capability profiles, telemetry payloads, and PSFN denial handling.
- Python tests for ESPHome fallback claim envelope construction, cert config, capability profile, and denial handling.
- Smoke fixture for the thin shell against a local PSFN shakedown runtime using a registered test endpoint.
- Fixture docs showing the matching PSFN registry entry needed for each test endpoint class.

Acceptance:
- npm run test:ts passes.
- uv run pytest passes.
- Smoke test can distinguish framework denial from hub transport failure.
- Examples remain secret-free and do not require real cert/private key material in the repo.
notes:
Partial progress during framework/hub compatibility pass: added TS and Python unit coverage proving the hub emits framework registry-protocol headers and canonical capability/telemetry mappings. Remaining scope: local PSFN shakedown smoke fixture and docs showing matching satellites.json entries for endpoint classes. Validation already run for this partial: npm run check:ts, npm run test:ts, PYTHONPATH=. uv run pytest.
Docs updated in docs/building-satellites.md with the current framework registry header contract, satellites.json examples, hub .env alignment, PSFN capability/telemetry mapping, and an Amica/Android alignment checklist. Remaining scope remains local shakedown smoke fixture coverage.

title: Forward configured satellite telemetry to PSFN
description:
Summary:
Add hub-side telemetry forwarding for endpoints that are configured and authorized to report prompt-enriching state to PSFN.

Scope:
- Support telemetry categories aligned with the framework plan: location, timezone, room/site, presence, battery/health, device status, avatar state, and future sensor/robotics state.
- Let endpoint profiles mark telemetry as static, periodically refreshed, event-driven, or unavailable.
- Include freshness timestamps and source endpoint identity with telemetry events.
- Rate-limit telemetry and avoid sending raw location or sensitive sensor data unless explicitly configured for that endpoint.
- Capture local artifacts for forwarded telemetry and PSFN denials.

Acceptance:
- Tests cover telemetry disabled by default, allowed health/presence telemetry, and allowed mobile location/timezone telemetry.
- Telemetry forwarding uses the same secure PSFN connection and endpoint identity as chat turns.
- Denied telemetry does not block ordinary voice/text turns unless configured as required.

title: Polish Device Studio preview layout
description: Move the Stack-chan Three.js physical preview into the primary left preview position, place the screen/sprite preview on the right, and remove explanatory note-style copy from visible page content so the studio feels like a tool surface instead of documentation.
close_reason: Polished Device Studio preview layout with primary left Three.js Stack-chan simulator, compact right sprite display, and no explanatory note copy in the page content.

title: Implement deterministic sprite-sheet packer for expressions and visemes
description: Implement the deterministic sprite-sheet builder that turns approved source frames into firmware-friendly avatar assets. It should support expression frames and speaking/viseme frames for small LCD devices, profile-specific target sizes such as 320x240 and 360x360 round-safe layouts, transparent/background handling, atlas packing, frame metadata, and future RGB565 conversion.\n\nThis task should be deterministic and testable without calling FAL. FAL-generated images are only one possible input source; manually supplied PNGs and cached generated outputs must follow the same manifest path.
acceptance_criteria: A CLI or Device Studio action can pack expression/viseme source PNGs into a sprite sheet plus JSON manifest for at least one device profile. The output records frame IDs, dimensions, safe area/round clipping metadata, source provenance, and content hash. Tests cover stable packing order, manifest schema, rejected malformed inputs, and behavior/profile compatibility.
close_reason: Completed deterministic expression/viseme sprite-sheet packer, CLI, RGBA PNG support, profile metadata, and tests.

title: Add server-side FAL image provider for sprite source generation
description: Add an optional server-side image generation adapter for Device Studio sprite source art using fal.ai. The provider should support configured model IDs for text-to-image and image-to-image/edit flows, starting with Nano Banana / Nano Banana 2 and GPT Image through FAL where available.\n\nThe adapter must read credentials from runtime environment such as FAL_KEY only. Do not commit keys, expose them to browser JavaScript, include them in logs, or store them in bead text. Generated outputs should include prompt, model ID, seed/options, source image references, and provenance metadata so later sprite exports remain auditable.
acceptance_criteria: A server-side FAL provider can call configured text-to-image and image-to-image/edit endpoints when FAL_KEY is present, returns typed image result metadata, and fails clearly when the key is missing. Tests use mocked fetch/client calls and prove the key is not serialized to browser bundles, logs, manifests, or event payloads. Docs explain FAL_KEY setup without containing a real key.
notes: FAL docs checked 2026-05-11: current image endpoints include fal-ai/nano-banana, fal-ai/nano-banana/edit, fal-ai/nano-banana-2, fal-ai/nano-banana-2/edit, fal-ai/gpt-image-1.5, and fal-ai/gpt-image-1.5/edit. Keep endpoint IDs configuration-driven so a future GPT Image 2 endpoint can be added without changing sprite pipeline code.
close_reason: Completed server-side FAL image provider with env-only credential handling, mocked tests, docs, and browser bundle exclusion.

title: Upgrade Stack-chan preview to Three.js physical motion renderer
description: Replace or augment the current CSS Stack-chan pose preview with a Three.js renderer that shows physical movement using the normalized Stack-chan model manifest. It should render the body/display/head assembly, apply head yaw and pitch from normalized behavior playback, show LEDs/status where available, and keep hardware-unverified motion warnings visible.\n\nThe renderer should run inside Device Studio tooling only and must not affect core hub runtime. It should gracefully fall back to primitive geometry if the physical model asset is absent or too heavy.
acceptance_criteria: Device Studio renders a nonblank Three.js Stack-chan scene, applies yaw/pitch behavior frames to the expected pivot groups, and keeps existing expression/viseme/display state visible. The scene is responsive, LAN-served dev mode still works, and there are deterministic checks for model loading/fallback plus at least one browser/canvas smoke check where practical.
close_reason: Completed required Three.js Stack-chan physical motion renderer with upstream STL case assets mounted into yaw/pitch groups, no CSS motion stand-in, LAN browser verification, and tests.

title: Add Stack-chan CAD asset intake and normalized 3D model manifest
description: Add a Device Studio asset intake path for the DIY Stack-chan physical case/model. The current repo checkout does not contain the STL/GLB/OBJ asset, so this task should define the canonical asset location, import the STL when available, record source/license/provenance, and normalize it for browser preview use.\n\nThe output should be a small manifest describing model parts, coordinate system, units, pivot points, and which submeshes map to head yaw, head pitch, display, body, neck, LEDs, and any fallback primitive geometry. Avoid committing huge generated binaries unless the repo policy explicitly allows it; prefer reproducible conversion scripts and ignored/generated output when appropriate.
acceptance_criteria: A documented asset path and manifest exists for Stack-chan 3D model intake. If the STL is present, a reproducible conversion/check command validates dimensions, orientation, and joint pivot metadata. Source/provenance/license are recorded. The Three.js renderer can consume the manifest or a deterministic fallback without blocking the app.
close_reason: Completed Stack-chan CAD intake manifest, canonical asset paths, pivot metadata, fallback geometry, docs, and tests.

title: Device Studio end-to-end QA and integration polish
description:
Final integration pass for the Device Studio epic. Run quality gates, smoke the live/mock flows, confirm the studio is visually usable across desktop/mobile-ish widths, verify behavior import/export, and ensure production hub behavior remains stable.

This is also the point to reconcile outputs from parallel worktrees, resolve naming inconsistencies, and update beads with any discovered follow-up work.
acceptance_criteria: All Device Studio beads are complete or have explicit follow-up beads. npm checks/tests pass or documented blockers exist. The studio launches, mock mode works without credentials, live mode can connect to the hub, docs are current, and git status is clean after commit/push.
close_reason: Completed final Device Studio QA: TypeScript check, unit/integration tests, studio build, server syntax check, diff check, LAN Node server, desktop/mobile Playwright smoke for CAD preview, behavior editor, sprite import, and sprite pack API.

title: Add Device Studio unit and integration test coverage
description:
Add focused tests for the Device Studio tooling: model validation, profile loading, behavior playback, import/export, mock/live transport message handling, and any protocol extensions. Include useful logging around simulated hub events and behavior playback so failures are easy to diagnose.

Keep tests compatible with the existing npm test/check flow and avoid requiring physical hardware or paid provider credentials.
acceptance_criteria: npm run check:ts and npm run test:ts cover the shared Device Studio model/transport logic. Any browser-only checks have a documented command and do not require hardware. Tests fail clearly on malformed profiles, invalid behavior frames, and websocket message regressions.
close_reason: Completed Device Studio unit/integration coverage across model, profiles, behavior playback/import-export, transport, previews, event log, and protocol decision tests; npm run check:ts, studio:build, and test:ts passed

title: Document Device Studio usage and hardware verification workflow
description:
Write the operator documentation for running Device Studio, selecting profiles, connecting live/mock transports, importing/exporting behaviors, and moving a behavior from host-generated to hardware-verified. Include guidance for Stack-chan intake and Waveshare round LCD intake.

The workflow should prevent accidental confusion between simulator-only movement and hardware-tested movement.
acceptance_criteria: README or docs updates include launch commands, profile descriptions, live/mock setup, behavior import/export examples, and a hardware verification checklist. Documentation calls out what is simulator-only versus tested on real devices.
close_reason: Updated README and Device Studio operator documentation with LAN launch, mock/live transport, behavior editing, sprite generation/import/approval/packing/export, FAL_KEY handling without secrets, and hardware verification workflow.

title: Document and test embodiment protocol extensions
description:
If Device Studio needs structured embodiment messages beyond the existing realtime protocol, define them explicitly and add tests. Candidate messages include embodiment.state or behavior.command events carrying expression, viseme, gaze, servo, LED, and display state.

Do not silently overload existing text/audio events with simulator-only meaning. Existing realtime clients must continue working. Any new message shape should be optional, capability-gated, and documented.
acceptance_criteria: Protocol docs and TypeScript types/tests exist for any added message. Existing protocol tests pass. If no extension is needed for the MVP, this bead should close with a note explaining why the current protocol plus local simulator state was sufficient.
close_reason: Documented Device Studio MVP uses existing realtime protocol plus local simulator state

title: Add live/mock app-server panel and structured command event log
description:
Implement the right-side operational panel inspired by the Stack-chan simulator screenshot: live/mock mode, connection status, send controls, generated draft/import actions, and an append-only command/event log. The log should show structured JSON events such as behavior frame application, behavior import/export, hub message receipt, interrupt, and hardware verification changes.

This panel should make it obvious whether events came from live hub traffic, mock traffic, user editing, or host-side generation.
acceptance_criteria: The studio exposes live/mock state, logs structured events with timestamps/source/profile/session where applicable, and supports sending a typed user turn through the selected transport. Event log output is testable and exportable or copyable for debugging.
close_reason: Completed live/mock operations panel and structured event log on agents/device-studio-logpanel

title: Add behavior authoring controls for joints, expressions, visemes, and timelines
description:
Add the editor controls needed to build or tweak behaviors inside Device Studio: select profile, choose expression/viseme, adjust joint values, add/update/delete timeline frames, duplicate behaviors, and mark generated behaviors as pending hardware verification.

Controls should be practical for repeated engineering use: sliders or numeric fields for joints, segmented/toggle controls for modes, profile-aware enabled/disabled states, and clear command buttons for import/export/play/duplicate/delete. The first screen should remain the studio workspace.
acceptance_criteria: A user can create a new behavior, edit frames, preview playback, duplicate existing behaviors, export JSON, and see unsupported controls disabled by selected profile. Generated behaviors retain provenance and hardware verification status.
close_reason: Implemented real Device Studio behavior authoring: create, duplicate, delete, rename, frame add/duplicate/delete/select, direct frame editing for expression, viseme, display, backlight, Stack-chan joints, LEDs, preview playback, provenance, and simulated-only verification state.

title: Render 2D LCD avatar and touch simulation
description:
Implement a profile-aware 2D display preview for ESP32 screen devices. It should render square and round displays at native aspect/shape, show expression/viseme states, and simulate touch events on profiles that support touch. This is the primary path for smaller LCD-only devices such as the Waveshare round board.

Use stable dimensions and responsive constraints so the preview does not resize unpredictably as states change. The renderer should be driven by the shared behavior playback state, not hardcoded UI-only state.
acceptance_criteria: The studio renders Stack-chan and Waveshare screen previews with expression/viseme changes, round-screen clipping for the Waveshare profile, and touch event logging. Tests or deterministic render-state checks cover profile-aware display sizing and channel degradation.
close_reason: Completed profile-aware 2D LCD preview and touch simulation on agents/device-studio-2d

title: Render Stack-chan 3D preview and joint visualization
description:
Implement the Stack-chan movement preview surface. It should visualize the body/screen and pan/tilt joint channels so authored behaviors can be inspected before hardware flashing. Use Three.js or a similarly appropriate 3D renderer if adding a web app renderer; keep the model lightweight and avoid blocking the MVP on perfect CAD fidelity.

The preview must make unverified hardware status visible and must not claim motion ranges are safe until real hardware intake confirms them.
acceptance_criteria: A Stack-chan preview responds to behavior playback frames and joint state. The preview can run in mock mode, has nonblank render verification where practical, and clearly marks hardware-unverified behavior. It does not affect core hub runtime.
close_reason: Completed Stack-chan motion preview and joint visualization on agents/device-studio-motion

title: Lowercase repository name and local path references
description: Rename the GitHub repository and local checkout path to lowercase, then update README absolute links to match the lowercase checkout path.
close_reason: Renamed GitHub repo and local checkout to lowercase; updated README path links

title: Update README for Voxta facade
description: Document the newly added Voxta-compatible hub facade, including supported routes, message coverage, config, limitations, and follow-up work.
close_reason: README updated with Voxta facade usage, configuration, limitations, and architecture notes

title: Add Voxta URL audio playback artifacts
description: Extend the Voxta facade to produce playback-ready local audio artifacts for VaM/Voxta clients, expose them over a hub route, and emit the Voxta speech/audio metadata expected by the AcidBubbles plugin.

title: Add Voxta vision upload handling
description: Implement the Voxta vision capture/upload path for VaM/Voxta clients and route posted images into the embodied session metadata/tooling surface without bypassing PSFN policy.

title: Add Device Studio sprite generation and review panel
description: Add a Device Studio workflow for generating, reviewing, approving, and packing sprite frames for ESP32 display profiles. The panel should let a user choose expression/viseme targets, provide prompts and optional reference images, request server-side generation, review returned candidates, mark approved frames, and pack/export sprite sheets.\n\nThe UI must make generation provenance and hardware/firmware readiness visible. It should never expose FAL_KEY to browser code; browser actions call a local server endpoint or CLI-backed tooling that owns the secret.
acceptance_criteria: Device Studio has a sprite workspace that can request generated candidates through the server-side provider, review cached/manual source frames, approve frames for named expressions/visemes, and invoke the deterministic packer. Missing FAL_KEY disables generation but still allows manual import and packing. Event logs record model ID/options/provenance without secrets.
close_reason: Implemented sprite generation/review workspace with server-side FAL generation endpoint wiring, manual PNG import, candidate approval/rejection, approved frame review, deterministic pack/export through the server packer, and secret-free event logging.

title: Add sprite and firmware asset export pipeline
description:
Add export tooling for assets that future ESP32 firmware can consume. The first target is small-screen avatar assets: PNG sprite sheets and/or generated RGB565-ready data for expressions/visemes, plus behavior JSON that can be embedded or served.

This should not require firmware integration yet. It should produce deterministic artifacts from the same behavior/profile model used by the studio so the emulator and firmware assets do not diverge.
acceptance_criteria: Export commands or UI actions can generate deterministic sprite/behavior artifacts for at least one profile. Outputs are documented, ignored or stored intentionally, and covered by golden or structural tests where appropriate.
notes: Expanded 2026-05-11: sprite export now depends on a server-side FAL provider for optional generated source art (psfn-bsy.18) and a deterministic sprite-sheet packer (psfn-bsy.19). Generated art must remain optional/cached/provenanced; firmware exports must be reproducible without network calls.
close_reason: Completed Device Studio sprite and firmware asset export path via UI pack/export, server-side pack endpoint, PNG atlas download, JSON manifest download, deterministic packer reuse, and documented CLI/UI workflow.
